action: Deploy TORWIND-2 (SATELLITE, solar/0-fuel) to scout all non-fuel markets in X1-PZ28 via 'workflow scout-all-markets --system X1-PZ28'
rationale: 0 credits, no cached market data (L2/L3/L4: free solar scout is the only source of price intel). EXECUTION BLOCKED this session: mutating CLI commands are not in the advisory-mode allowlist (dontAsk denies them). Queued as plan-of-record for when actuation is permitted.
expectation: Once executed: a scout-tour container RUNNING for TORWIND-2 and cached market data for every non-fuel-station waypoint in X1-PZ28 within 2h.

action: Run 'workflow batch-contract --ship TORWIND-1 --iterations 5' on the COMMAND ship (40 cargo, 400 fuel) for bootstrap income
rationale: Treasury is 0 credits; contracts pay on acceptance AND delivery (L6), guaranteed income to seed capital before any speculative trade. EXECUTION BLOCKED this session by advisory-mode permissions. Queued as plan-of-record.
expectation: Once executed: credits > 0 within 2h (first contract acceptance payment) and a batch-contract container RUNNING for TORWIND-1.

action: Now that phase-2 unblocked mutating CLI commands and the market/ledger DB recovered, execute plan-of-record: launch scout-all-markets (TORWIND-2) AND batch-contract 5 iters (TORWIND-1) together.
rationale: Actuation confirmed working; DB back (player/market/ledger respond, no SQLSTATE). Treasury is actually 176,547 credits, not 0 (ledger balance after +1,547 acceptance = 176,547; the report's 0 was stale from last session's DB outage).
expectation: Both containers RUNNING; market data cached and contract progressing within 2h.

verdict: Firing two heavy workflows at once transiently hung the daemon socket (context deadline exceeded, ~2min of failed health checks); the scout coordinator died in that window (0 scout-tour containers spawned) and batch-contract failed unrecoverably at iteration 0 with 'cannot plan purchase of IRON_ORE: no profitability/market data available (scout markets first)'. Daemon self-recovered once load dropped.
lesson: Launch heavy workflows one at a time and confirm daemon health between; batch-contract needs cached market data, so scout must run and gather data first.

action: Re-launch scout-all-markets for X1-PZ28 ALONE on the recovered daemon (container scout-tour-TORWIND-2-65007a67, RUNNING 0/inf).
rationale: Scout data is the prerequisite for both the stalled IRON_ORE contract and any trade route. Single launch on a healthy daemon avoids the overload that killed the first attempt.
expectation: Cached market data for the non-fuel-station waypoints in X1-PZ28 accumulating; >=3 markets cached within 2h and the IRON_ORE export market identified.

action: Defer re-running batch-contract on TORWIND-1 until the scout has populated market data; leave TORWIND-1 idle-with-reason (awaiting scout intel). The accepted IRON_ORE contract (+1,547) stays active and must be fulfilled next session.
rationale: Re-running now would fail again (no market data) and churn the daemon. Cheaper to wait for intel than to thrash. Deadline risk exists but is unobservable (no contract-list command).
expectation: Next session, with market data present, re-run batch-contract on TORWIND-1 -> IRON_ORE sourced, delivered, contract fulfilled, second payment received.

action: Continue letting the solar scout (TORWIND-2, scout-tour-...-65007a67) complete its 26-market tour of X1-PZ28; keep TORWIND-1 docked idle-with-reason (awaiting an IRON_ORE-selling market). Do NOT re-run batch-contract until an IRON_ORE market is confirmed in cache.
rationale: 3/26 markets scanned (A1 consumer goods, H65 raw ice/silica exchange, H66 precious metals) - none sell IRON_ORE. Re-running batch-contract now fails fast (L21). Scout is free (solar) and healthy; cheapest path to intel is to wait one lap, then decide: buy-and-deliver if IRON_ORE market found, else pivot to mining for the contract.
expectation: Within ~40min the scout covers most of the 26 markets; either an IRON_ORE purchase market is identified (then batch-contract TORWIND-1) or none exists (then plan a mining op). Verdict at next heartbeat: is IRON_ORE purchasable in X1-PZ28?

action: Split scouting: assign idle TORWIND-1 (speed 36) a single scout-markets tour over the far half of the 26-waypoint route (K90,A2,A3,A4,C42,D45,D46,E48,E54,F56,F58,F59,F60) while TORWIND-2 continues its infinite tour from the near end. Launched as ONE workflow on the healthy daemon (L22).
rationale: The solar scout (TORWIND-2, speed 9) has cached only ~5 of 26 markets across 3 sessions; at that rate the IRON_ORE contract stays blocked indefinitely and the fast command ship sits idle. Two ships converging from opposite ends ~halves time-to-full-coverage and puts the idle ship to work. Fuel burn (~68/unit, refuelable at the many fuel waypoints) is trivial vs 176k treasury.
expectation: Both ships RUNNING; >=10 new markets cached within ~40min; IRON_ORE purchase market identified (or proven absent -> pivot to mining). TORWIND-1 tour completes, freeing it for contract duty.

verdict: Solo scout launch kept the daemon healthy the whole run; market coverage went from ~5 to 18/26 cached and B7 was found to sell IRON_ORE @48. Expectation met.
Deferring batch-contract until data existed was correct: the one time it fired early (22:18, before the scout completed at 22:29) it failed identically. Re-running now with data present (d-8).
Core question answered: IRON_ORE IS purchasable in X1-PZ28 (B7 @48, MODERATE, vol 180) - no mining pivot needed. The 'wait one solar lap' plan was overtaken by d-7's faster split scout.

Split scout succeeded: TORWIND-1 far-half single pass COMPLETED (workflow.finished success), lifting coverage to 18/26 and surfacing the IRON_ORE source at B7; command ship freed for contract duty. Surprise: batch-contract was (re)launched at 22:18 BEFORE the scout completed at 22:29, so it failed on stale/absent data - a sequencing slip, not a scout-split failure.
lesson: When splitting scout + contract in one session, the contract MUST wait for workflow.finished on the scout, not just for the scout container to be RUNNING - container-RUNNING != data-populated.

action: Re-run batch-contract on TORWIND-1 (iterations 1) now that IRON_ORE is cached as purchasable at X1-PZ28-B7 (@48). Single launch; TORWIND-2 scout keeps running (L25: 2nd workflow on a healthy 1-container daemon is safe).
rationale: The accepted IRON_ORE contract (+1,547 acceptance) is still active and owes a 2nd fulfillment payment. Prior failures were purely missing market data; that prerequisite is now satisfied (18/26 markets, IRON_ORE source confirmed). Purchase cost is trivial (~48/unit x <=40 cargo = <2k/trip, far under the 50% guardrail on ~175k).
expectation: Container reaches delivery/fulfillment: TORWIND-1 navigates to B7, buys IRON_ORE, delivers to the contract waypoint, contract fulfilled with a CONTRACT_FULFILLED ledger entry. Watch for a repeat 'no profitability/market data' failure (would indicate a freshness filter rejecting 3h-old cache).

action: Record daemon socket-hang incident and DEFER contract re-verification. No actuation possible this session: the socket path (health/ship/container/workflow) hung with 'context deadline exceeded' on 16 consecutive probes over ~5min and did not self-recover, while the DB-backed path (ledger) stayed responsive (L19). Both containers (scout-tour-TORWIND-2, batch_contract-TORWIND-1) died; batch-contract restart e1871c14 had resumed the IRON_ORE contract, re-confirmed profitability, and was navigating TORWIND-1 to buy when a 'Route segment execution failed / context canceled' (23:04) killed it. Cannot restart daemon (no CLI verb; process control denied) - relying on self-recovery per playbook.
rationale: Socket subsystem is hung but daemon is partially alive (DB path works). No Captain-side restart exists; hand-probing health burns tokens without advancing anything. Correct move is to record the incident with signature+evidence, mark socket actuation degraded, and let the daemon self-recover between sessions. IRON_ORE contract is still active/unfulfilled (no CONTRACT_FULFILLED / PURCHASE_CARGO in ledger; treasury 175,251 unchanged since 19:36).
expectation: Next session: socket path responds to health; re-launch batch-contract on TORWIND-1 to finish the IRON_ORE contract (2nd payment => CONTRACT_FULFILLED ledger entry) and re-launch scout-all-markets on TORWIND-2. If the socket is STILL hung at next session start, that is a 3rd occurrence of the hang signature => write reports/bugs/ escalation.

verdict: Re-confirmation (already closed s5): the core question - is IRON_ORE purchasable in X1-PZ28 - was answered YES (B7 @48). No further action needed.

batch-contract did NOT fulfill: ledger still 7 txns (no PURCHASE_CARGO / CONTRACT_FULFILLED), treasury frozen at 175,251. The workflow logic was sound (profitability re-confirmed, purchase initiated) but the socket-hang bug killed the nav leg at 23:04:02Z before any cargo was bought. Failure is environmental (daemon), not strategic.
lesson: A workflow.finished 'success' event is NOT proof of completion - cross-check the ledger for the expected PURCHASE_CARGO/CONTRACT_FULFILLED rows. Here 'success:true' was emitted for a container that bought nothing.

verdict: Deferral was correct and its predicted branch fired: the socket was STILL hung at s7 start (3 probes, all context deadline exceeded; DB path alive). Per the pre-staged plan this is the 3rd occurrence of the hang signature -> escalated to reports/bugs/ this session. Nothing lost by deferring since no Captain-side restart exists.
lesson: Confirmed: the daemon socket hang is a RECURRING bug, not a transient fluke - 3 sessions (s2 recovered ~2min, s6 >5min no recovery, s7 still hung). Stop treating each hang as novel; it is a filed defect until the fix pipeline resolves it.

action: Escalate the daemon socket-hang to reports/bugs/2026-07-02-daemon-socket-hang.md (status:new, kind:fix) - 3rd occurrence of the same signature (s2/s6/s7) per playbook threshold. Mark socket actuation DEGRADED in strategy.md and end the session; no actuation is possible (every ship/container/workflow verb needs the hung socket).
rationale: Playbook: SAME failure signature 3+ times across sessions -> STOP retrying, write bug report, work around it. The condition d-9 pre-staged (socket still hung at next session start) is met. Hand-probing health burns tokens without advancing anything (loops/sleep/Monitor denied in dontAsk mode, L30). The fix pipeline auto-picks up status:new reports.
expectation: The fix pipeline lands a daemon change isolating the socket listener from container teardown. Next session: if health responds, re-launch batch-contract on TORWIND-1 (finish IRON_ORE, expect CONTRACT_FULFILLED) then scout-all-markets on TORWIND-2. Verdict: is the socket responsive at s8 start, and did the report change status from new?

verdict: Re-listed by the harness but already resolved (s5/s7): IRON_ORE is purchasable at X1-PZ28-B7 @48. No open question remains; no action.
Re-listed but already resolved (s6): the split scout completed, lifted coverage to 18/26, and surfaced the B7 IRON_ORE source. Scouting is no longer the blocker; the daemon socket hang is. No action.

action: Record 4th consecutive daemon socket-hang and DEFER again. s8 start: health + container list both returned 'context deadline exceeded'; DB path (ledger) answered instantly - still 7 txns, treasury 175,251 frozen since 19:36Z, IRON_ORE contract still unfulfilled. Bug report 2026-07-02-daemon-socket-hang.md remains status:new (fix not yet landed). Appended s8 as a 4th occurrence to the report. No actuation possible; no Captain-side restart exists.
rationale: Playbook + L30: known filed defect, not a novel incident. Every ship/container/workflow verb needs the hung socket; hand-probing burns tokens (loops/sleep/Monitor denied). Correct move is record, keep the bug report's occurrence log current, and end. The fix must come from the daemon-side fix pipeline.
expectation: Fix pipeline lands a daemon change isolating the socket listener from container teardown, flipping the report off status:new. Next session: probe health; if responsive, re-launch batch-contract on TORWIND-1 (finish IRON_ORE -> CONTRACT_FULFILLED) then scout-all-markets on TORWIND-2. Verdict: is the socket responsive at s9 start, and did the report leave status:new?

verdict: Waiting for scout intel was correct: IRON_ORE market found (B7), and batch-contract eventually purchased 40 units. Re-listed by harness; already resolved prior sessions.
Split-scout achieved coverage; IRON_ORE purchase market confirmed and 40 units bought. Re-listed by harness; already resolved.

action: Socket RECOVERED (health ok). Re-launch batch-contract on TORWIND-1 (--iterations 1) to finish the active IRON_ORE contract. Ship is DOCKED at delivery waypoint X1-PZ28-H63 with 40/40 IRON_ORE already purchased (PURCHASE_CARGO -2080). Prior container e1871c14 FAILED on a transient cargo desync (API 4219: reported 0 units while ship holds 40), caused by the socket hang mid-delivery.
rationale: Goods are bought and the ship is already at the delivery point; the only missing step is deliver+fulfill. The desync was hang-induced; ship info now reads clean 40/40. Error 4219 is a NEW signature (not the socket hang), first occurrence, so one retry is warranted before any escalation.
expectation: Within ~10min: a CONTRACT_FULFILLED row appears in the ledger (treasury jumps above 172,451) and the batch-contract container reaches COMPLETED. If it re-crashes with the same 4219 desync, that's signature hit #2 -> stop and diagnose, do not loop.

verdict: Re-launch reproduced the exact 4219 desync deterministically (container b47f99e2, 4 retries, FAILED). The 40/40 IRON_ORE in ship info is PHANTOM: the server deliver endpoint authoritatively reports 0 units. Not hang-induced debris but a purchase/cargo consistency bug on a freshly-restarted daemon.
lesson: When ship info disagrees with a server-side API error about the same cargo, the SERVER is authoritative; the daemon cache is the liar. Do not retry a workflow that fails on server-reported cargo=0 - it is deterministic, not transient.

action: Sell-probe: attempted `ship sell TORWIND-1 IRON_ORE 10` at H63 (which buys IRON_ORE @134) to test phantom-vs-real cargo and possibly recover stranded capital. It CRASHED the CLI with a nil-pointer SIGSEGV in APIMetricsCollector.RecordRateLimitWait (api_metrics.go:134). Filed reports/bugs/2026-07-02-ship-sell-nil-panic.md and reports/bugs/2026-07-02-phantom-cargo-contract-delivery.md (both status:new, kind:fix). Marked contract-fulfillment + sell DEGRADED; no more batch-contract re-launches on TORWIND-1 until the daemon reconciles ship state.
rationale: Both of TORWIND-1's cargo-offload paths are now proven broken (contract delivery: deterministic 4219; manual sell: hard crash). Continuing to actuate TORWIND-1 is pure waste. The productive move is to escalate the two distinct, reproducible defects to the fix pipeline and defer TORWIND-1 until a daemon restart re-fetches true ship state. Scout TORWIND-2 stays running (healthy, solar, free).
expectation: Fix pipeline picks up both status:new reports. Next session: if the daemon has been restarted, re-check TORWIND-1 cargo via ship info - expect it to read 0/40 (phantom cleared) once reconciled; if so, re-negotiate/re-run a contract cleanly. If ship info STILL shows a phantom 40 that the server rejects, the reconcile fix has not landed - do not re-launch, just note recurrence.

verdict: Scout deployed and productive across all sessions: TORWIND-2 scout-tour has been RUNNING (solar, free, restart_count 0), its metadata carries all 26 X1-PZ28 markets on an infinite tour, and it surfaced the IRON_ORE source at B7. Market intel is now self-healing at zero cost.

By its own stated expectation d-2 worked: batch-contract negotiated+accepted an IRON_ORE contract (CONTRACT_ACCEPTED +1,547, credits went >0) and the container ran. The fulfillment half is a SEPARATE saga (d-8/d-12/d-13) blocked by the phantom-cargo daemon bug, not by this decision.
Re-listed by harness; long-resolved. IRON_ORE is purchasable at X1-PZ28-B7. No open question.
Re-listed by harness; long-resolved. Split scout lifted coverage and surfaced B7; scout now self-covers all 26 markets. No action.

Re-listed. The batch-contract purchase it authorized is the origin of the phantom cargo: PURCHASE_CARGO -2,080 ledgered but the server never added the goods (cargo=0). Purchase/cargo-consistency defect, not strategy. Tracked under the phantom-cargo bug report.

Re-listed. Confirmed deterministic: the phantom 40/40 IRON_ORE survived socket recovery, the d-12 relaunch (container b47f99e2, events 31-35), AND into s10 - ship info still reads 40/40 while the server rejects delivery (4219, cargo=0). No Captain verb reconciles it; only a daemon restart/fix will.

action: HOLD session. Keep TORWIND-2 scout RUNNING (healthy, solar, free, self-covering all 26 markets). DEFER TORWIND-1: the phantom 40/40 IRON_ORE cargo persists (server says 0). Take NO actuation on TORWIND-1 - do not re-launch batch-contract (deterministic 4219, L32), do not ship sell (segfault, L33), do not navigate/orbit/dock to 'force a refresh' (those return nav+fuel only, never cargo - L34). Do NOT buy a replacement hauler to work around it.
rationale: Both TORWIND-1 offload paths are proven dead and no Captain-accessible verb overwrites the daemon's cargo cache - only a daemon restart re-fetches true ship state. The phantom-cargo bug (status:new, not yet picked up by the fix pipeline) blocks ALL purchase-then-deliver revenue flows, so buying a fresh hauler would just expose new capital (86k guardrail) to the same unfixed consistency bug and risk bricking a 2nd ship (L16: validate before scaling). The disciplined move is to hold: keep the free scout building intel, wait for the phantom-cargo fix to land, then resume.
expectation: Next session: (a) check phantom-cargo report status - if it left status:new the pipeline has picked it up; (b) re-read TORWIND-1 ship info - if cargo reads 0/40, the phantom cleared (daemon restarted/reconciled) -> re-run a clean batch-contract to finish the IRON_ORE contract; if still 40/40, note recurrence and keep holding. Scout stays RUNNING throughout.

action: Close d-10 (socket-hang escalation).
verdict: Socket recovered and stayed healthy s9-s11 (the 'socket responsive' half of the expectation was met), but NOT via a pipeline fix: the operator addendum reframed the multi-session blackout as a manual-restart PID-lock race, not the filed code bug. The report never left the unfixed track - it went new->gate_failed(s10)->new(s11). The escalation targeted a non-bug; recovery was an ops artifact.
lesson: L30/L35 already cover this - an escalation can chase a phantom code bug when the true cause is an out-of-band ops event, and gate_failed is not terminal (a report can revert to new).

action: HOLD (s11) - identical posture to d-14. Keep TORWIND-2 scout RUNNING; DEFER TORWIND-1 (phantom 40/40 IRON_ORE persists into a 3rd session, server=0). No actuation on TORWIND-1; do not buy a replacement hauler.
rationale: State is an exact repeat of s10: daemon healthy, phantom cargo unchanged, all 3 bug reports still status:new (phantom-cargo blocker unpicked for a 3rd session; socket-hang reverted gate_failed->new). No Captain verb reconciles the cargo cache (L34); both TORWIND-1 offload paths are dead (L32/L33). Holding costs ~0 (treasury flat at 172,451, idle ships don't bleed). Throwing a fresh hauler at an unfixed purchase/cargo-consistency bug risks bricking a 2nd ship (L16/d-14).
expectation: Next session: (a) phantom-cargo report status - if it left new, pipeline picked it up; (b) TORWIND-1 ship info - if cargo reads 0/40 the phantom cleared -> re-run a clean batch-contract to finish IRON_ORE; if still 40/40, keep holding. Scout stays RUNNING.

action: Close d-14 (s10 HOLD).
verdict: The HOLD's else-branch fired exactly as predicted: at s12 ship info still reads 40/40 phantom IRON_ORE (server=0), so 'keep holding' was correct. Holding cost nothing (treasury flat at 172,451, 11 txns unchanged) and avoided gambling capital on an unfixed defect. The blocker itself did not clear (phantom-cargo report still status:new), which was outside the decision's control.

action: Close d-15 (s11 HOLD).
verdict: Exact repeat of d-14: s12 is identical (daemon healthy, phantom 40/40 persists, treasury flat, phantom-cargo report still status:new for a 4th session while the socket-hang report re-touched to gate_failed). The predicted 'still 40/40 -> keep holding' branch held. No capital lost; no progress on the external blocker.

action: HOLD (s12), 4th consecutive. Keep TORWIND-2 scout RUNNING (healthy, solar, free). DEFER TORWIND-1 (phantom 40/40 IRON_ORE, server=0). Do NOT buy a replacement hauler. EXIT CONDITION: if phantom-cargo is still status:new at the next meta-review, promote fix-pipeline priority-ordering as the top instrument-panel gap so HOLD does not run unbounded.
rationale: Re-read the phantom-cargo report: the desync posted at 23:16:55Z on a FRESHLY-RESTARTED, HEALTHY daemon (restart 23:16:14Z), NOT during a socket hang. So it is a purchase/cargo consistency defect on a healthy daemon, making the ENTIRE purchase-then-deliver revenue CLASS unreliable, not a one-off hang artifact. Buying a ~50k hauler would therefore buy an UNRELIABLE revenue path: even if one purchase landed, the next could desync and brick the new ship (L16/L32). Holding is ~free (idle ships don't bleed) and trivially reversible; a bricked ship is not. Decisive: 50k for a path that may desync on any purchase is a bad trade.
expectation: Next session: (a) TORWIND-1 ship info - if 0/40 the phantom cleared (daemon restart/fix) -> run ONE clean batch-contract to finish IRON_ORE (expect CONTRACT_FULFILLED, treasury >172,451); if still 40/40, keep holding. (b) phantom-cargo report status - if it left 'new', the pipeline picked it up. Scout stays RUNNING.

action: Close d-16 (s12 HOLD).
verdict: TORWIND-1 still reads phantom 40/40 (server=0) so the predicted keep-holding branch fired; phantom-cargo report still status:new (5th session unpicked). SURPRISE this session: the free scout TORWIND-2 went DOWN on a NEW signature (API 4204 nav-to-destination) — a daemon POSITION cache desync (daemon H64, server H65), same root class as the phantom cargo. The d-16 exit condition (promote fix-pipeline priority-ordering at the next meta-review) remains pending; phantom-cargo is still new.
lesson: L37: the daemon ship-state cache desync is NOT cargo-specific — it also corrupts POSITION and crash-loops the scout. It is a whole-cache-consistency defect, not one phantom field.

action: Recover crash-looped solar scout TORWIND-2: manually 'ship navigate' it to a THIRD waypoint (H66 — not the stale-cached H64, not the phantom 'already-at' H65), which reconciled the position cache; then relaunch scout-all-markets for X1-PZ28.
rationale: Scout was down on a NEW signature (API 4204 position desync). Unlike the cargo desync (L34, not Captain-recoverable), a navigate to a third waypoint executes from the server's TRUE position and forces the daemon to re-read/reconcile on arrival. Cheap, reversible experiment; the scout is my only free always-on intel asset ('no ship idle >60min without reason').
expectation: H64->H66 navigate succeeds with no 4204 (CONFIRMED: ship info read H66 IN_ORBIT); relaunched scout-tour-...-48adae90 RUNNING and progressing, no 4204 (CONFIRMED). Next session: scout still RUNNING, restart_count low, cache refreshing; if it 4204-crashes on a later hop, repeat the third-waypoint navigate. Filed reports/bugs/2026-07-03-scout-position-cache-desync.md (status:new).

action: HOLD (s14), 6th consecutive on TORWIND-1. Keep TORWIND-2 scout RUNNING (healthy, solar, free, restart_count 0, no 4204). DEFER TORWIND-1 (phantom 40/40 IRON_ORE, server=0). Still NO hauler purchase.
rationale: MATERIAL CHANGE: phantom-cargo report left status:new -> awaiting_human. The fix pipeline finally worked the critical blocker (5 sessions unpicked) and PROPOSED a fix branch, now gated behind the user's manual merge (propose-only mode, captain.auto_merge:false). So the d-16 exit condition (promote priority-ordering if still 'new') is MOOT. But the fix is NOT merged and the daemon has NOT restarted, so ship info still reads phantom 40/40 and the purchase-then-deliver class stays unreliable -> buying a ~50k hauler still risks bricking a 2nd ship (L16/L32). HOLD is ~free (treasury flat 172,451; idle ships don't bleed) and reversible. The correct move is to wait for the user to merge the proposed fix + a daemon restart, then verify ship info reads 0/40 before any fresh contract.
expectation: Next session: (a) TORWIND-1 ship info - if 0/40, the fix merged + daemon restarted -> run ONE clean batch-contract to finish IRON_ORE (expect CONTRACT_FULFILLED, treasury >172,451); if still 40/40, keep holding. (b) phantom-cargo report status - awaiting_human means fix proposed/pending user merge; if it goes merged/fixed the fix landed. Scout stays RUNNING.
